finding: NOT a headless-divergence bug (minimize aborted: engine==FCP-headless at the worst frame, 99 dB). The 10.97 dB gap is engine-vs-GUI-GT ONLY. Census: group 'Cartoon Whoosh' (out=0.467) > image 'A' (transitionA, DZ type=1 1920x1080, RETIME extrap=1, A.out=0.434) carrying Directional Blur + Scrape(PAEScrape/Smear) filters, and image 'Drop Zone' (transitionB, DZ type=2 4096x2160, RETIME extrap=1, B.out=0.467). endSec=1.134. BOTH drop zones wrap (extrap=1) and die well before endSec. The A-filter (Scrape displacement + Directional-Blur blow-out) dissolves A away over the first third, revealing B underneath. GUI trajectory: A f00-06 (131,83,54) -> dark smear collapse f07-16 (~76,43,40) -> B fades in f17-23, SETTLES on photo B (92,106,137). HEADLESS FCP: A f0-4 -> jumps to B ~f5 and HOLDS B to end (settles on B, no wrap-to-A). ENGINE (pre-fix): wrapped to frame-0 (frozen photo A 131,85,56) for the whole tail f09-23 -> WRONG (GUI holds B). The prior 'small box / drop-zone scale' hint was NOT the bug (both drop zones conform full-frame correctly; the Scrape filter math is already verified vs headless).
decoded_geometry: Scrape(PAEScrape) is an inverse-map displacement warp (Center rel[0..1]->px, Rotation=3pi/2 axis +X, Amount=70 static -> threshold a=130) whose Center-X curve only animates t=0.77-0.87 (in the wrap region). Directional Blur Amount keyframes t=0.801->1.134->1.201 (also wrap region). So the smear/blur are authored to fire in the WRAP tail. B has no filters; static full-frame op=1. A/B retime: A kf t=0:1 -> t=1.235:38 (extrap wrap), B kf t=0:1 -> t=0.467:15.
fix: hasFilterRevealSettleB capability probe (capabilities.ts) fires on {Smear, Bloom} (2-slug family, no-hardcode >=2): EXACTLY 2 Retime-wrap zones both Transition drop zones (A+B), Transition-A carries >=1 image filter (the reveal driver), NO overlay media/generator, and Transition-B dies before endSec (B.out < end-1frame; distinct from pureCrossfadeSettleB which needs B alive to end). For this family: (1) timemap.ts keeps the wrap-to-frame-0 for the MID-transition frames (where GUI's dark/blown transitional state is colour-closer to frozen warm A than to cool B ~11.4 dB/frame) but RELEASES the wrap for the final tail (settleBSec = endSec*0.72) so natural scene time resumes and the drop-zone crossfade settles on photo B; (2) evaluator/index.ts sets holdIncomingB + a NEW ctx.filterRevealSettleB flag so the incoming B drop zone is HELD opaque past its `out` and forces its timed-out parent group visible (heldIncomingB) — without which the wrap-released tail would render BLACK (both drop zones die at 0.467s). Light Noise's existing holdIncomingB (blended-overlay case) is untouched: heldIncomingB is gated on filterRevealSettleB (false for Light Noise).
gate_result: GATE GREEN: 0 regressions, 2 improvements vs baseline_engine.json (tol 0.3dB). Movements__Smear 10.97->11.67 (+0.70), Lights__Bloom 10.55->11.38 (+0.83). settleBSec swept 0.68-0.87: 0.72 chosen as the balanced midpoint (Smear crosses to B ~0.80*end, Bloom ~0.67*end). All other 63 slugs byte-identical (change gated on hasFilterRevealSettleB={Smear,Bloom}); Lights__Light_Noise confirmed neutral (17.0 vs 17.07, within noise+TOL). tsc --noEmit clean; no-hardcode.test.ts green (hasFilterRevealSettleB fires on 2); evaluator/parser/all-transitions/integration unit tests pass.
